JScience 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT
  1. org.jscience.social.sociology
  2. Situation

Uses of Class
org.jscience.social.sociology.Situation

Packages that use Situation
Package
Description
org.jscience.social.economics
Provides comprehensive mathematical models for economic systems, agent-based modeling, and financial analysis.
org.jscience.social.law
Provides the org.jscience.social.law module.
org.jscience.social.linguistics
Provides mathematical and computational models for natural language processing, linguistic analysis, and glottochronology.
org.jscience.social.medicine.consultation
Core functionality for the org.jscience.social.medicine.consultation package in the JScience library.
org.jscience.social.politics
Provides the org.jscience.social.politics module.
org.jscience.social.sociology
- social structures, persons, and cultural modeling.

  • Uses of Situation in org.jscience.social.economics

    Subclasses of Situation in org.jscience.social.economics
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    EconomicSituation
    Represents a social situation involving the interaction of people around economic resources and activities.
    class 
    WorkSituation
    Represents a work situation where individuals interact around a common economic activity, typically at dedicated workplaces.

  • Uses of Situation in org.jscience.social.law

    Subclasses of Situation in org.jscience.social.law
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    LawSuitSituation
    Represents the process and context of a lawsuit or trial.
    class 
    StreetSituation
    Represents the context for interactions between individuals and the legal system in a public or "street" level context (e.g., citizenship, public order).

  • Uses of Situation in org.jscience.social.linguistics

    Subclasses of Situation in org.jscience.social.linguistics
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    ChatSituation
    Represents the interaction of participants communicating.

  • Uses of Situation in org.jscience.social.medicine.consultation

    Subclasses of Situation in org.jscience.social.medicine.consultation
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    MedicalSituation
    A class representing the interaction of people around a common activity or conflict.

  • Uses of Situation in org.jscience.social.politics

    Subclasses of Situation in org.jscience.social.politics
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    CivilSituation
    Represents a social interaction context where individuals interact as citizens within urban or civil environments. * @version 1.1
    class 
    Election
    Represents a political election event, aggregating votes for candidates.
    Constructors in org.jscience.social.politics with parameters of type Situation
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    Candidate(Individual individual, Situation situation, String office)
    Creates a new Candidate role for an individual.

  • Uses of Situation in org.jscience.social.sociology

    Subclasses of Situation in org.jscience.social.sociology
    Modifier and Type
    Class
    Description
    class 
    Celebration
    Represents a cultural or religious celebration (feast, rite of passage, festival).
    class 
    Ritual
    Represents a formalized traditional behavior or sequence of actions (e.g., handshaking, bowing).

    Field
    Description
    static final Situation

    Methods in org.jscience.social.sociology that return Situation
    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Situation
    Role.getSituation()
    Returns the situation context for this role.
    Constructors in org.jscience.social.sociology with parameters of type Situation
    Modifier
    Constructor
    Description
     
    MasterOfCeremony(Individual individual, Situation situation, Organization organization)
    Creates a new MasterOfCeremony.
     
    Role(Individual individual, String name, Situation situation, int kind)
    Deprecated.
    Use Role(Individual, String, Situation, RoleKind) instead
     
    Role(Individual individual, String name, Situation situation, RoleKind kind)
    Creates a new Role for an individual within a specific situation.
